fact: January 21 is exactly one month after the winter solstice, which means days are getting noticeably longer in the Northern Hemisphere - you gain about 2 minutes of daylight every single day!
In Antarctica right now, it's the middle of summer and the sun never sets! Penguins are having 24-hour daylight parties while you're bundled up in winter coats.
This is prime time for the weirdest weather phenomenon ever: thundersnow! Lightning and thunder can happen during heavy snowstorms, creating an eerie green glow in the sky.
Squirrels are going absolutely nuts right now - literally! They're frantically digging up their buried acorn stashes because their winter fat reserves are running low.
The zodiac sign switches from Capricorn to Aquarius around January 20-21, making this the cosmic border between the stubborn goat and the quirky water-bearer.
Tree sap is completely frozen solid in cold regions, which is why you hear those sharp CRACK sounds when tree branches snap - they're basically giant wooden popsicles!
January 21 falls exactly in the middle of the coldest stretch of winter in most northern places - meteorologists call this the 'dead of winter' for a reason!
Bears in hibernation are doing something gross and amazing right now: they're not pooping at all! Their bodies create a special butt plug made of hair and plant material.
The moon phase around this date changes every year, but when there's a new moon in late January, you can see the most stars because there's zero moonlight pollution in the sky.
Evergreen trees are actually working overtime right now! Their needles are photosynthesizing in the weak winter sun while all the other trees are taking a months-long nap.
This date marks the peak of 'lake effect snow' season near big bodies of water - warm lake water meets freezing air and creates snow monsters that can dump 6 feet overnight!
Owls are at their loudest and most active right now because it's mating season! Those spooky hoots echoing through winter nights are basically bird love songs.
